sanskrit:
दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तव सौम्यं जनार्दन।
इदानीं अस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvedaṃ mānuṣaṃ rūpaṃ tava saumyaṃ janārdana / idānīṃ asmi saṃvṛtaḥ sacetāḥ prakṛtiṃ gataḥ //
literal_meaning: Having seen this human form of Yours, so gentle, O Janārdana, now I am composed, conscious, and restored to my own nature.
contextual_meaning: After the traumatic vision of the cosmic form, Arjuna’s nervous system down-regulates when he again sees Krishna’s familiar human shape; his sense of self re-integrates and he reports relief, coherence, and a return to baseline identity.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic stabilization; secure-base re-attunement; shift from hyper-arousal to ventral-vagal social engagement.
trigger_scenario: After an intense psychedelic or near-death experience, the person sees a trusted friend’s face and suddenly feels 'back in their body' and okay again.
anchor_text: Familiar presence calms the mind and restores your true self.
